title: AsRef and AsMut
description: The `AsRef` and `AsMut` traits allow cheap reference-to-reference conversions.
instructions: Implement the function using AsRef to accept both String and &str.
hints: AsRef<str> lets a type provide a reference to str cheaply.
Add the bound: fn byte_counter<T: AsRef<str>>(arg: T)
explanation: The `AsRef<T>` trait provides a cheap reference-to-reference conversion. By using `T: AsRef<str>`, the function accepts any type that can be viewed as a `&str`, including `String`, `&str`, and `&String`. This is a common pattern in APIs to make them flexible. `AsMut` is the mutable equivalent.
